Active in Labs

Colby

Offensive Security & Active Directory Specialist

I'm a California-based cybersecurity student specializing in Active Directory exploitation, web application testing, and enterprise network attack vectors. I spend my time building vulnerable environments, cracking active directory labs, and drafting highly detailed, production-grade technical writeups.

⚡ Active Directory Lab Explorer

Click through the interactive nodes below to visualize the multi-stage attack chains used to compromise these Active Directory target domains.

Initial Credential Foothold

Assumed Breach

The pentest operates under an assumed breach scenario, beginning with a valid standard domain user account to emulate an internal threat or successful phishing landing.

Compromised Target faraday (Domain User)
Primary Tool NetExec (nxc)
Command Executed
nxc smb 10.0.30.225 -u faraday -p hacksmarter123

Core Competencies & Toolbelt

My technical competencies mapped directly to real-world exploits, security frameworks, and professional pentesting utilities. Click any tag to highlight connected concepts.

👑 Active Directory Security
Kerberoasting AS-REP Roasting ADCS ESC1 Shadow Credentials DCSync (Secretsdump) LAPS Password Theft GenericAll / GenericWrite
🛠️ Pentesting Toolbelt
BloodHound NetExec (nxc) Certipy pywhisker Responder Evil-WinRM Hashcat Burp Suite
🖥️ Operating Systems & Security
Windows Server Active Directory Domain Kali Linux Registry Hives Dump DPAPI-NG Decryption DCC2 Cached Creds
💻 Scripting & Engineering
Python (dpapi, scripts) PowerShell / Cmdlets Bash Scripting Command Injection CVE-2025-24054 Exploitation Git / GitHub Pages
